text: This will be a discovery research interview, and the questions will be open-ended.
There are no right or wrong answers, so answer as honestly as you can.
Are you ready to get started?
Yes.
To start, could you tell me about a specific situation where using an ATM felt like the only or best option for you?
Using an ATM when I had to get money out for like, for, um, what was it, like school for my kid or whatever.
I felt that was the best option for me is because I was in a hurry.
Like when I'm in a hurry, I'll go to the ATM if I need cash.
Could you walk me through that specific situation with getting money out for school?
What was happening that put you in a hurry?
The time.
I was running late.
And what were you running late for in that moment?
I was running late for my kid's school to bring him to school.
What made going to the ATM the best choice when you were running late to take your child to school?
Because he needed cash for school and I had to use the ATM to get cash out because I don't really carry cash, so I needed that ATM.
What did you need the cash for at school?
Extended day.
Can you tell me more about what extended day means in this context and why it required cash?
Extended day is like after-school care, and it required cash because they make you pay them for extended day after-school care.
And how did you feel in that moment, knowing you needed cash for extended day and were running late?
[Speaker:JASMINE JASMINE] I mean, it was helpful to use the ATM when I needed cash and I was running late at the same time.
It sounds like the ATM helped you out of a tight spot.
Can you describe another time when an ATM was the best or only option for you?
The ATM was the only best option for me because the bank wasn't even open at that time.
I had to get cash, so the ATM came helpful.
Can you tell me about a specific time when the bank was closed and you needed cash, so you had to use the ATM?
On a holiday, it was closed and I needed cash, so I had to use the ATM for that.
Which holiday was this, and what did you need the cash for on that particular day?
[Speaker:JASMINE JASMINE] It was on Christmas, and I needed cash for that day for gas.
[Speaker:INTERVIEWER] So, on Christmas Day, the bank was closed, and you needed cash for gas.
How did it feel to rely on the ATM in that situation?
[Speaker:JASMINE JASMINE] It felt beneficial.
I'd like to understand your experiences with cash.
Can you tell me about a recent time you used physical cash?
Um, a recent time I used physical cash was, like I was saying, extended day.
I had to get cash out of the ATM so I could pay my kids' school.
Thanks for sharing that.
Can you walk me through your thinking process that led you to choose cash for that payment rather than another method?
Because that was the only method I was able to use.
I wasn't able to just use a credit card to pay it.
They wanted actual cash.
That makes sense.
So, the school specifically required cash.
How did that make you feel?
Having to go to an ATM specifically for that purpose?
It made me feel okay.
I mean, I didn't feel no certain way of having to go to ATM to get cash out.
That's good to hear.
You mentioned this was for an extended day at school.
Can you tell me a bit more about what Extended Day is and why cash is the required payment method for it?
Extended Day is after-school care, and cash is a required payment because they want you to pay for cash.
They want you to give them cash.
Credit cards is not an option.
I don't know why, but they just require cash.
Cash is just the only method.
Thank you.
To start, could you tell me about a time you used an ATM that belonged to a bank where you don't have an account?

Yes, I needed cash at my actual bank, and in that town they didn't have the, uh, the bank that I use, so I had to go to somebody else's bank to get cash out of it, and it charged me like a fee, like a $3 or $4 fee.
Thank you for sharing that experience.
When you realized you had to use an bank's ATM and saw the fee, how did that make you feel?
[Speaker:JASMINE JASMINE] It didn't make me feel good at all because I didn't want to, you know, have to have extra money.
I didn't want to have to spend a processing fee just to get money out of the bank.
[Speaker:AI RESEARCHER] I understand it was frustrating to pay an extra fee.
Can you tell me more about what specifically made you feel it was extra money you you didn't want to spend?
Because it was like a few dollars here or there, and I didn't want to pay that.
I see.
You felt that even a few dollars was an unnecessary expense.
Did you have any thoughts about the bank that charged the fee, or the bank you normally use, at that moment?
No.
Okay.
So your feelings were more about the fee itself, rather than the banks involved.
Can you recall another time you used an ATM that wasn't from your primary bank?
No.
No.
We're going to shift our focus a bit now.
I'd like to understand how consistent you feel a bank's digital experience, like their mobile app or website, is with their physical ATM experience.
Could you tell me, how do you perceive the consistency between a bank's digital presence and its ATM experience?
How I perceive the consistency between a bank's digital presence and its ATM experience.
That's a good question.
I'm not really sure.
That's perfectly fine.
We can explore it together.
To start, when you think about a bank's mobile app or website, what comes to mind regarding its visual design and how it functions?
The visual design and how it functions.
What comes to mind?

Is like how the app is organized and like how easy it is to access, you know, like if it has a chat feature on it that I can chat with someone if I'm having issues with the app, you know, stuff like that comes into mind.
That's helpful.
So ease of access and features like a chat function are important to you in the digital experience.
Now, thinking about the ATM experience, what comes to mind regarding its visual design and how it functions?
When an ATM, like how it functions, what comes to mind with an ATM is like, let's see, like The features on it, like what features am I able to use?
Like, am I able to get out this certain amount of cash versus this amount?
Stuff like that.
So for ATMs, the specific features available, like cash withdrawal limits, are important to you.
Do you recall a time when you noticed a clear consistency or inconsistency between your bank's app and one of its ATMs?
Either in terms of how it looked or how it worked?
No, not really.
That's okay.
Let's try a different angle.
When you use your bank's mobile app, what kind of overall feeling or impression does it give you?
The impression that it gives me with my bank's mobile app is it makes me feel good.
That's interesting.
You mentioned your bank's mobile app makes you feel good.
Could you tell me more about what specifically contributes to that positive feeling when you use the app?
Is when I'm logging in and my information is saved.
I don't have to keep getting them to send me a code to like log in, or like if I have the Face ID on, it's easier to log in like that.
It sounds like the ease of logging in, especially with features like Face ID, really contributes to that good feeling you get from your mobile app.
Now, shifting to the ATM experience, what kind of overall feeling or impression do you get when you use an ATM from your bank?
The feeling or impression I get when I use the ATM, I mean, it's neutral.
You describe the feeling from using your bank's ATM as neutral.
Can you explain a little more about what you mean by neutral in this context?
I mean, like, it's good and bad.
It's good, but, you know, it's not perfect, but it's not bad either to use it.
Okay, so it's not a strong positive or negative experience.
Could you give me an example of something about your bank's ATM experience that might lean towards the good side for you?
Might lean towards the good side of me is giving me no issues at the ATM.
Like, I'm getting my cash out, you know, it's fast, it's easy.
So, a fast, easy transaction with no issues when getting cash out is a good aspect of the ATM experience for you.
What about something that might lean towards the not-perfect side, or perhaps a minor frustration when using your bank's ATM?

A minor frustration or like something happening with the ATM, like where it freezes up, it doesn't give me my money, and like it starts circling around, like loading, acting like it's going to go through and it doesn't.
That's definitely frustrating when an ATM freezes or doesn't dispense cash smoothly.
Does the visual design of the ATM, like the screen layout or colors, ever contribute to that feeling of frustration, or does it feel consistent with your bank's other branding?
No, it feels consistent with my brand's branding.
To start, how do you see the ATM fitting in with your overall digital banking experience?
Good question.
I'm not sure.
That's perfectly fine.
Let's think about it this way.
When you use your banking app on your phone, what are some of the things you wish you could also do or finish at an ATM?
Some things that I wish I could do or like finish at an ATM is like having the chat agent on an ATM since I have it on like my actual app, banking app.
That would be helpful if it was on an ATM as well.
That's an interesting idea.
Can you tell me more about why having a chat agent directly on the ATM would be helpful for you?

Having a chat agent, like, sorry, on the ATM would be helpful for me is because like if I'm having issues with the app, you know, and there's a line behind me, I can just hurry up and go do that, um, chat agent thing and they can help me out instead of having to sit there and wait, listen to someone on the phone.
That makes sense.
So if you're having trouble with your banking app while at the ATM, having a chat agent there could help you resolve it quickly without needing to call someone.
Can you describe a specific time when you were at an ATM and encountered an issue where a chat agent on the machine would have been particularly useful.
A chat agent on the machine would have been helpful and useful whenever the ATM started like freezing up and it started doing like a little circle thing loading and it never even gave me my money.
Like it said on my app that they took out the money, but it didn't dispense the money.
Thank you for sharing that specific example.
It sounds like a very frustrating experience when the ATM froze and didn't dispense your money.
How did that situation make you feel at the moment?
[Speaker:JASMINE JASMINE] Made me feel very angry and frustrated.
Given that feeling of anger and frustration, how would having a chat agent on the ATM in that moment have changed your experience?

It would have changed my experience because if they could help me out, you know, and maybe there was like a reset button on the ATM or something, I could just press it and start all over and it would dispense like, oh, that would be amazing.
That would be awesome.
You know?
That's a clear vision for how it could be better.
So if the chat agent could have guided you to a solution like a reset button, it would have resolved your anger and frustration.
Can you think of any other specific features or ways the ATM could connect with your digital banking experience to make it feel more modern and integrated beyond the chat agent?
No.
No.
Thank you so much for sharing your thoughts and experiences today.
